channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Nanoporosity-driven deformation of additively manufactured nano-architected metals
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69845-8

A two-photon-lithography hydrogel-infusion process prints 3D nickel nano-architectures with ~100-nm features and high specific strength, indicating that localized nanoporosity hotspots govern deformation and size-dependent strength.
item.dc:title: Nanoporosity-driven deformation of additively manufactured nano-architected metals
item.dc:creator: Wenxin Zhang
Zhi Li
Huajian Gao
Julia R. Greer

item.title: Polyanion-stabilized amorphous halide electrolytes with low lithium content for all-solid-state lithium batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69737-x

Halide solid electrolytes typically require high lithium content, raising costs and exacerbating air sensitivity. Here, authors leverage anion clusters to synthesize an amorphous electrolyte with low lithium content of 2.4 wt%, high ionic conductivity of 1.5 mS cm-1, and good air-stability.
item.dc:title: Polyanion-stabilized amorphous halide electrolytes with low lithium content for all-solid-state lithium batteries
item.dc:creator: Wen Tang
Feilong Wang
Shuaika Liang
Fiaz Hussain
Jo-chi Tseng
Pengcheng Yu
Jiuwei Lei
Hailun Jin
Chunlei Zhao
Haochang Zhang
Zhepu Shi
Ying Li
Wen Yin
Fucheng Ren
Shuo Wang
Zi‑Feng Ma
Xueliang Sun
Wei Xia

item.title: Strong optical anisotropy in one-dimensional phosphorus wavy tubes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70129-4

Strong optical anisotropy is commonly achieved by complex engineered materials. Here, authors synthesize elemental crystals of type-II red phosphorus made of wavy tubular structures, exhibiting strong birefringence in the visible and near-infrared spectral region. They demonstrate anisotropic photoluminescence, Raman scattering and second-harmonic generation.
item.dc:title: Strong optical anisotropy in one-dimensional phosphorus wavy tubes
item.dc:creator: Shuai Zhang
Zhaolong Liu
Tongtong Jiang
Chen Wang

Feng Ding
Xue-Feng Yu
Zhipei Sun
Jiahong Wang

item.title: Vertical nanodiamond dominated sheets possessing both high capacitance and high n-type Hall mobility
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70089-9

The authors transformed Ta-loaded vertical graphene into diamond by Ar/O plasma treatment at ordinary pressure and obtained vertical nanodiamond dominated sheets electrode with both high capacitance and high n-type Hall mobility.
item.dc:title: Vertical nanodiamond dominated sheets possessing both high capacitance and high n-type Hall mobility
item.dc:creator: Yuemin Gong
Zhiqiang Zhang
Meiyan Jiang
Chengke Chen
Shaohua Lu
Xiaojun Hu
